data.title: agent worktree dispatch: isolation:worktree branches from stale orchestrator HEAD -> later agents in serialized chain conflict on overlapping files at harvest
data.description:
## Incident Overview
Sprint 2026-04-19 (epics ca48-c3c3 + db68-ca95) hit 3 separate merge-conflict episodes during per-worktree harvest because `Agent isolation: worktree` creates each agent worktree from the orchestrator's CWD HEAD at the moment of dispatch — NOT from the latest session branch HEAD at harvest time.

## Reproduction Cases (this session)
1. **Batch 4a (a1a5-a42e Pattern Reference impl + a0f42d4c MAX_ATTEMPTS sprint Phase 4)**: aaa722c0 worktree branched from session HEAD before commits 2ff2937d (Consumer Detection) and 7408e37f (Retry Budget) had been harvested. When aaa722c0 was harvested, its Pattern Reference subsection conflicted with the existing Step 3 modifications. Resolution required manual conflict resolution preserving all 3 subsections.
2. **Multiple deep-tier impl tasks (ab01-e486, 9b97-76ac, 87f4-e93f, 267d-ec8e, 45c0-02f1)**: Each agent worktree branched from old HEAD without sibling reviewer-delta files (or test files), forcing each agent to manually `git checkout worktree-20260418-215740 -- ...` to restore session-side changes before regenerating agents via build-review-agents.sh.
3. **End-session merge of origin/main**: 10 reviewer agent files conflicted because both main and our session regenerated them. Required manual resolution (take ours, then re-run build-review-agents.sh).

## Pattern
The serialized chain pattern: when N implementation tasks must complete in sequence (T1→T2→T3) and each task touches the same file, dispatching all in parallel with worktree isolation creates N parallel worktrees branched from the same stale base. The first to land succeeds cleanly; the rest conflict at harvest. Even when properly serialized (dispatch T2 only after T1 lands), there's a window between "T1 harvested" and "T2 dispatched" where T2 branches from session HEAD — but session HEAD updates from in-flight harvests can still cause stale snapshots if dispatch happens before the harvest commit lands.

The chained-impl-tasks-on-same-file pattern is common in the planning pipeline epic (5 reviewer-delta-* tiers all add `## AI Blindspot Annotations` to siblings; impl-plan SKILL.md Step 3 grew 5 new subsections in different tasks).

## Cost
- ~30+ minutes of orchestrator time on manual conflict resolution across 3 episodes
- Each agent had to spend tool calls on `git checkout` to restore session-side state before they could verify GREEN
- Multiple "is_merged" false positives confused harvest reporting (related: bug 1eda-6a0c)

## Expected Behavior
When dispatching with `isolation: worktree`, either:
1. The worktree is created from the latest session branch HEAD at the moment the Agent tool call executes (not from a snapshot taken at orchestrator dispatch instruction), OR
2. The harvest protocol detects branch staleness and re-bases or re-runs the agent against the new session HEAD before merging

## Actual Behavior
Worktree branches from a stale snapshot — timing-dependent on dispatch ordering vs orchestrator state.

## Suggested Fixes
1. **Late-binding worktree creation**: defer worktree branch creation until just before agent execution, using session HEAD at that moment (rather than at orchestrator dispatch time)
2. **Pre-harvest rebase**: in per-worktree-review-commit.md, rebase the agent branch onto current session HEAD before merging (small cost, eliminates stale-base conflicts)
3. **Build-script idempotency advisory**: when an agent task involves running build-review-agents.sh (or similar regenerators), document that the worktree must be rebased onto session HEAD or the regenerated outputs may overwrite sibling work

## Workaround
Either:
- Serialize impl tasks that touch the same file (1 at a time), and verify session HEAD has the sibling commit before dispatching the next
- Tell agents to run `git checkout worktree-<session> -- <sibling-file>` before regenerating shared outputs
- Manually resolve at harvest time and run regenerators in session worktree

## Related
- 1eda-6a0c (P2): orchestrator cleanup destroys unharvested work when in-worktree commit gate fails — both this and 1eda-6a0c are symptoms of weak coupling between agent worktree branches and session HEAD
- d888-632b (P2): harvest "already merged" false-negative — same family
